english: lates to a certain medical procedure or method of curing.
(Ssk. प्रियङ्गु) millet, k're-čáṅ (khre chang) Murwa-beer Sik., v. Hook. Himal. Journ.

resp. t'ugs-k'rél (thugs khrel) 1. shame, shamefacedness, bashfulness, modesty; *ṭel káb-če* W. v. gébs-pa (gebs pa). — 2. piety, esp. W. — 3. C. disgust, aversion. Comp. and deriv. — k'rel-gád (khrel rgod) a scornful laughter. — k'rél-čan (khrel can) Cs. bashful, timid; W. pious, faithful, conscientious. — k'rel-ltás (khrel ltas), k'rel-ltos (khrel ltos), dread of wicked actions; k'rel-gdoṅ (khrel gdong) (lit. a face capable of shame) id. — *ṭél-dad-čan, ṭél-dod-čan* W. fond of making others ashamed. — k'rel-ldán (khrel ldan) = k'rél-čan (khrel can). — k'rél-ba (khrel ba) vb. to make or to be ashamed, *ṭel ṣoṅ* he was ashamed, *ṭél-če mi yoṅ* W. he is not put to shame; C. also: to get into a passion; sbst. shame, k'rél-ba (khrel ba) daṅ (dang) ṅo-tsa-ba (ngo tsha ba) med (med) he has no shame nor dread Dzl., *ṭél-wa yod* W. it is a shame. — k'rel-méd (khrel med) (-pa), W. *k'rel-méd- (kan)* shameless, insolent. — *ṭél-go* object of disgust, C. — k'rél-yod (khrel yod) chastity, modesty, decency, k'rél-yod-pa (khrel yod pa) chaste etc., k'rél-yod-par byéd-pa (khrel yod par byed pa) to behave chastely etc. — k'rel-šór (khrel shor) = k'rel-méd (khrel med).

1. a kind of bronze, of about the same quality and worth as °k'ár-ba ('khar ba), but inferior to li (li), q.v.; k'ró-ču (khro chu) liquid, melted bronze; k'ró-čus sdóm-pa (khro chus sdom pa) to fill up joints, grooves etc. with melted bronze, to solder Glr. — 2. kettle Sch.

1. anger, wrath, (cf. k'ró-ba (khro ba) vb.) frq: kóṅ-k'ró-ba (khong khro ba) inward anger Thgy. — 2. angry, wrathful Cs.; k'ró-bar byéd-pa (khro bar byed pa), gyúr-ba (gyur ba) to be, to grow angry Cs.; k'ró-bo (khro bo), fem. k'ró-mo (khro mo) angry, fierce, ferocious, e.g. gčaṅ-gzán (gcan gzan) a ferocious beast; esp. applied to the 54 (or 60) deities of anger and terror (भैरव), e.g k'ró-ba-čén-po (khro ba chen po) = gšiṅ-rje (gshin rje) the ruler of hades; *ṭo-túm-po* furious with rage, raging with anger C.: k'ro-gnyér (khro gnyer) distortion of the face by anger: k'ró-ba-ma (khro ba ma), k'ró-ba-čan (khro ba can) she whose face is wrinkled with anger, n. of a goddess Glr. 17, 12. — *ṭo-ṭá* W. dissatisfaction, grumbling. — k'ro-món (khro mon) Sch. prison (perh. k'ri-món (khri mon)). — k'ro-žál (khro zhal) an angry, frowning countenance Glr.

? — k'rog brgyáb-pa (khrog brgyab pa) to drink hastily, to gulp down Glr.; k'rog-k'rog (khrog khrog) plump! the sound caused by something falling heavily on the ground W. — k'rog-smán (khrog sman) the raw, unprepared substance of a medicine Sch.
botanical term, used of leaves standing round the stem scattered or alternately.
upright, straight, erect, (cf. kroṅ (krong)), Glr., Mil.

, W. *ṭó (-pa)* crowd, assemblage, mass, multitude; mi-k'ród (mi khrod) a troop, crowd of men, ri-k'ród (ri khrod) an assemblage (mass) of mountains; rtsva-k'ród (rtsva khrod) a heap, stack, rick of hay; nags-k'ród (nags khrod) a dense forest, mun-k'ród (mun khrod) thick darkness; dur-k'ród (dur khrod) cemetery where the corpses are cut into pieces for the birds of prey; dei k'ród-du lha-gčíg jóg-pas (de'i khrod du lha gcig 'jog pas) placing the princess among their (the girls') company Glr.; in W. *ṭó-pai naṅ-na* and *naṅ-du* c. genit. is the usual expression for among.
claw, fang; k'rón-kyis rkó-bai sde (khron kyis rko ba'i sde) the class of the gallinaceous birds S.g.

1. well, spring. — 2. Lh.: a wooden water kennel; k'rón-bu (khron bu) a little well; also n. of a medicinal herb, a purgative against bilious complaints Med. k'ron-rágs (khron rags) enclosure of a well Sch.

1. market-place, market-street, bazar; k'rom skór-ba (khrom skor ba) to wander, to ride round the market Glr., to ramble through; gsaṅ-sṅágs k'róm-du klog (gsang sngags khrom du klog) secret spells (magic formulas) are read in the market (a crime and sacrilege in the eyes
